evals.prompt: Stop the remote workflows on suxrouter so I can do some local surgery without the pipeline fighting me — I'll tell you when to turn them back on.
evals.expected_output: Names exactly which loop file(s) it's disabling in `SuxOS/suxrouter` (e.g. `issue-build.yml`, `automerge.yml`) rather than a vague 'paused the pipeline', and explicitly reminds that a disabled cron is a standing change that needs re-enabling — doesn't just disable and move on silently. Also holds any in-flight PRs that must not move during the surgery.

evals.prompt: ok the surgery's done, re-enable everything
evals.expected_output: Re-enables the same specific workflow file(s) it (or a prior turn) disabled — not a generic/different set — and removes any holds it applied, using `gh workflow enable` / `gh pr edit --remove-label hold` rather than assuming the loop resumes on its own.

evals.prompt: a PR got stuck as needs-human on the fixer loop, can you get it moving again?
evals.expected_output: Requeues via removing the `needs-human` label so `pr-unstick` retries, rather than force-merging, force-pushing, or manually editing the PR's code. Treats this as a reversible label-edit action it can apply boldly, and doesn't confuse it with disabling/toggling a whole loop cron.
